lines.en_lit: of the heifer weight ten zuz; and of the scapegoat weight of two sela, and of the leper weight of a shekel,

Rabbi Yoḥanan Rabbi Shimon ben Ḥalafta and the Rabbis disagree of heifer. One said: weight of ten And one said: weight of one shekel. And your mnemonic both the one who gives much and the one who gives little

Rabbi Yirmeya of Difti said to Ravina: not with regard to the heifer they disagree; rather, with regard to the scapegoat they disagree. And on that day Ravya bar Kisi and they made a mnemonic out of it, Ravya bar Kisi atones like the scapegoat,
Rabbi Yitzḥak said: I heard two slaughters: One of the heifer, and one of the bull one is valid by a non-priest, and one is invalid by a non-priest. But I do not know which of them
was stated: slaughter of the heifer and the bull Rav and Shmuel: One said: heifer is invalid, bull is valid. And one said: His bull is invalid, heifer is valid.
Conclude it is Rav who said the heifer is invalid, as Rabbi Zeira said: The slaughter of the heifer by a non-priest is invalid. And Rav said concerning this we learned in “Elazar “statute”
And Rav, what is different heifer, that, it is written “Elazar” and “statute.” the bull it is also written “Aaron” “statute”
slaughtering is not service; If so, heifer also heifer is different. of consecrated for Temple maintenance,
is it not an a fortiori inference, Rav Shisha, son of Rav Idi, said: just as in appearances of leprosy, which, not service, require the priesthood.

And to Shmuel, who said the bull is invalid, what is different his bull that, it is written “Aaron,” and “statute,” heifer it is also written: “Elazar” and “statute” It is different there as it is written: “And he shall slaughter it before him” that it could be a non-priest slaughters and Elazar watches.
And Rav, that he should not divert his attention from And from where does Shmuel, that he should not divert his attention He derives it “And he shall burn the heifer in his sight”
And Rav one with regard to slaughtering, and one with regard to burning.

And necessary since, if the Merciful One had written it with regard to slaughtering, because it is the start of the service; but burning, I could say no, necessary And if the Merciful One had written with regard to burning, because it is now that heifer is being made ready but slaughtering, I could say no, necessary
to exclude what If we say, to exclude gathering its ashes and filling the water for sanctification,
